behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver -> vulnerability-check handoff across modules, not pure single-function logic. A real OS/network/subprocess is not required by the behavior text; an in-process fake vulnerability API can verify every extracted direct+transitive dependency is queried. Rejected System because the behavior does not require real HTTP/subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing known direct and transitive deps, with a fake vulnerability API endpoint/client that records requested package@version entries; assert the recorded query set exactly matches expected dependency closure.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about real query/response correctness against vulnerability DB semantics for version ranges. A mock can hide contract mismatches (wrong query params, server-side interpretation differences), so real HTTP/API interaction is needed to catch real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real (or provider sandbox) vulnerability API, submit dependencies with package names shared across vulnerable/non-vulnerable version ranges; assert returned CVEs correspond only to versions/ranges declared in the manifest and exclude out-of-range matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering policy with no module handoff dependency required to trigger failure. No real infra or AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call sorting function with unsorted CVE fixtures spanning all severities and tied severities with different CVSS scores; assert exact ordered output (Critical>High>Medium>Low, then CVSS descending within same severity).

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic manifest edit logic for a single dependency change; failure can be caught at function/object boundary without real filesystem/network. Rejected Integration because no cross-module/infra requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Given manifest text/object fixture with many dependencies, apply bump for one package and assert only that package version changed while all other keys/values remain byte-for-byte (or structurally) identical per format guarantees.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository PR creation and real external API/git interactions. In-process fakes can miss auth/branch/commit/diff/PR API integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, run scanner path that triggers auto-fix; assert a PR is created remotely, and diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated file modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is local deterministic policy logic. No real infra or model output required.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate semver comparison helper with cases like 1.x->2.x (breaking), 1.2->1.3 (non-breaking), pre-release/build metadata variants; assert breaking-change boolean is correct.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in orchestration/state handoff between semver decision, PR-creation module, and report/output module. Real OS/network is not required to prove this behavior; mock PR client should show no call while output contains review flag. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating remote PR API behavior, only that flow gates correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner service with semver detector + fake PR client + report collector; feed dependency requiring major bump; assert PR client was not invoked and scanner output includes breaking-change review flag.
